name: Seafood Enchiladas with Cream and Tomatillo S
recipeIngredient: 1/2 package Mexican Natural Cheese (or Farmers Cheese), 7.5 oz
3 Scallions, chopped with some of the green tops
1/4 cup Margarine or butter (you can use non-fat)
10 Fresh Tomatillos or more, to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the filling: In a food processor, cream cheeses until smooth. Transfer to a bowl and add remaining ingredients, stirring gently.
2. Prepare the Cream Sauce: Melt butter in a saucepan and stir in garlic and onion until transparent. Add flour and stir to make a roux. Gradually add liquids (margarine, yogurt, sour cream, evaporated milk) and stir gently until thickened. Stir in half a bunch of chopped cilantro. Do not use salt.
3. Prepare the Tomatillo Sauce: Peel skins from tomatillos and wash them. Place in boiling water in a saucepan and cook until tender with onion. Add salt and pepper to taste. Transfer mixture to a food processor, adding liquid if needed.
4. Assemble enchiladas: Place a dollop of filling on each flour tortilla and fold into a small package by folding the long ends together first, then the short ends, seam side down. Continue until all filling and tortillas are used.
5. Cover with Cream Sauce.
6. Top with Tomatillo Sauce.
7. Sprinkle chopped red bell pepper or pimiento on top of Tomatillo Sauce to decorate.
8. Bake at 350°F until bubbly and cheese is heated through. These enchiladas are low in fat and highly flavorful.
